# Honeybees

Honeybees are social insects that live in colonies of tens of thousands. A colony
has a single queen, many female workers, and seasonal male drones. Workers
communicate the location of food using a movement called the waggle dance.
Honeybees produce honey from flower nectar and store it for winter. As they
gather nectar they pollinate plants, making them vital to agriculture. The queen
is the only bee that lays fertilized eggs.
